items.q: How do you say 'I am going to visit Spain' in Spanish?
items.why: Students drop the 'a' or use a conjugated verb. The near future is IR (conjugated) + A + INFINITIVE. The 'a' is obligatory: voy A comer, vamos A ver, vas A estudiar. Heavily tested for holiday/future plans.
Students apply English pronunciation. Spanish H is ALWAYS silent. 'Hola' is 'OH-lah', not 'HO-lah'. (The breathy sound students associate with H comes from j or g+e/i in Spanish.)

items.q: Why does 'Me gusta el chocolate' literally translate as 'Chocolate is pleasing to me'?
items.correct: Gustar works backwards - the thing liked is the subject, the person is the indirect object.
items.misconception: Gustar means 'to like' and works exactly like English.
items.random_wrong: It is a typo for 'Yo gusto'.
items.why: Students translate gustar as 'to like' with English structure. Actually gustar = 'to be pleasing to'. So 'me gustan los libros' (books are pleasing to me) - the verb agrees with the thing, not the liker.

items.q: Which is correct: 'next to the cinema'?
items.why: Correct answer: al lado del cine. “al lado de el cine” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
It's one can / you can. “it must” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: If 'entre la estación y la iglesia' is between 2 places, how many places are mentioned?
items.why: The real figure is 2. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
